steps.title: Find where the two graphed lines intersect
steps.content: The graph shows a horizontal line at $y = -6$ and a second line passing through $(0, -6)$ and $(6, -5)$. Both lines pass through $(0, -6)$, so that is their point of intersection.

steps.title: Test whether the third line passes through (0, -6)
steps.content:
For all three equations to share a solution, the third line $x - 6y = -36$ must also pass through $(0, -6)$:
$$0 - 6(-6) = 36 \neq -36$$
The third line does not pass through $(0, -6)$, so the three lines have no common point. The system has zero solutions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
